title: Best Vagus Nerve Exercises: Ranked by RCT Evidence (2026)
description: Fifty-two popular vagus nerve exercises circulate online. Five have actual randomized controlled trial evidence. This ranking covers what the research shows for HRV, anxiety, and parasympathetic activation — and names the 47 that don't make the cut.
seoTitle: Best Vagus Nerve Exercises: Ranked by RCT Evidence (2026)
seoDescription: Best vagus nerve exercises ranked by RCT evidence for HRV and anxiety. The 5 that work (dose, timing, effect sizes) and why the other 47 are pseudoscience.
imageAlt: Anatomical vagus nerve rendering with HRV waveform overlay on a dark observation chamber CRT portal

ranked.name: HRV biofeedback training
ranked.summary: Hedges' g = 0.83 for anxiety in meta-analysis of 24 RCTs. Largest sustained evidence base. 5–20 min/day at resonance frequency (6 bpm).

ranked.name: Slow exhalation breathing (4:8 or 5:5 pattern)
ranked.summary: Consistent HRV increase across multiple RCTs. Gerritsen & Band 2018 review confirmed parasympathetic activation. Zero cost, no equipment.

ranked.name: Cold face immersion (diving reflex)
ranked.summary: Documented parasympathetic bradycardia response. RCT evidence for acute anxiety reduction. Combines with breathwork in polyvagal protocols.

ranked.name: Transcutaneous auricular VNS (taVNS)
ranked.summary: Liu 2023 meta-analysis: 12 RCTs, 838 participants, significant depression/anxiety improvement. Requires device. Cymba conchae targeting essential.

ranked.name: Humming / sustained vocalization
ranked.summary: Stimulates vagus nerve via laryngeal branch. Limited RCT evidence; mechanistically plausible. 2022 pilot data on HRV improvement.
